mission.title: Install and Configure Kubeedge on Kubernetes
mission.description: KubeEdge is a Kubernetes Native Edge Computing Framework that extends container orchestration and device management to edge hosts. Installing KubeEdge allows you to efficiently manage edge applications and devices while ensuring reliable communication between the cloud and edge environments.

steps.title: Add the KubeEdge Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official KubeEdge Helm repository to your Helm client to access the KubeEdge charts.
```bash
helm repo add kubeedge https://charts.kubeedge.io
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install KubeEdge using Helm
steps.description:
Install KubeEdge with the specified version, creating a namespace for it.
```bash
helm install kubeedge kubeedge/kubeedge --version v1.22.1 --namespace kubeedge --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the KubeEdge pods are running successfully in the kubeedge namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kubeedge
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the KubeEdge components to ensure optimal performance.
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment kubeedge --namespace kubeedge --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi
```

steps.title: Port-forward to access KubeEdge services
steps.description:
Use port-forwarding to access KubeEdge services locally for testing.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/kubeedge-cloudhub --namespace kubeedge 8080:8080
```

uninstall.title: Remove the KubeEdge Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the KubeEdge release using Helm.
```bash
helm uninstall kubeedge --namespace kubeedge
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) created by KubeEdge.
```bash
kubectl delete crd devices.kubeedge.io
kubectl delete crd nodes.kubeedge.io
kubectl delete crd pods.kubeedge.io
```

uninstall.title: Remove the KubeEdge namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the namespace used for KubeEdge to clean up all associated resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace kubeedge
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Export the current configuration and resources for backup before upgrading.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace kubeedge -o yaml > kubeedge-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure the Helm repository is up to date before upgrading.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade KubeEdge
upgrade.description:
Upgrade KubeEdge to the latest version available in the Helm repository.
```bash
helm upgrade kubeedge kubeedge/kubeedge --namespace kubeedge --version v1.22.1
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the KubeEdge pods are running successfully after the upgrade.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kubeedge
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If KubeEdge pods are stuck in a Pending state, check for resource availability and scheduling issues.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace kubeedge
```

troubleshooting.title: CloudHub not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot reach CloudHub, verify the service is running and check the logs for errors.
```bash
kubectl logs <cloudhub-pod-name> --namespace kubeedge
```

troubleshooting.title: EdgeCore not connecting to CloudHub
troubleshooting.description:
If EdgeCore fails to connect to CloudHub, check network policies and firewall settings.
```bash
kubectl logs <edgecore-pod-name> --namespace kubeedge
```

troubleshooting.title: DeviceController not managing devices
troubleshooting.description:
If DeviceController is not managing devices, check the CRDs and ensure they are properly configured.
```bash
kubectl get devices.kubeedge.io --namespace kubeedge
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of KubeEdge will have all pods running in the kubeedge namespace, with no errors reported in the logs. You should be able to access the CloudHub service and manage edge devices effectively.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add kubeedge https://charts.kubeedge.io
helm repo update
helm install kubeedge kubeedge/kubeedge --version v1.22.1 --namespace kubeedge --create-namespace
kubectl set resources deployment kubeedge --namespace kubeedge --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher and have Helm and kubectl installed.
